page: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage, which the instance is capable of supporting. There are no integrated GPUs on this instance, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D2ls_v7 is positioned as a low-memory option within its family. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient choice for compute-focused workloads that do not require large memory footprints or local scratch space. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, microservices, basic CI/CD tasks, and entry-level utility workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D2ls_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine within the Dlsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and is configured with 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. Lacking complimentary public IPv4 addresses, the server is designed as a low-memory, compute-oriented resource. The hardware profile is optimized for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of large memory capacities. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and basic utility tasks.
